source_intake_record.raw_question_text: The solid line and dashed line in the figure represent the wave profiles at \( t = 0 \) and \( t = 0.1\,\mathrm{s} \), respectively. It is known that the particle at the equilibrium position \( x = 6\,\mathrm{m} \) does not change its direction of motion during the time interval from \( 0 \) to \( 0.1\,\mathrm{s} \). What is the wave speed?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'in the figure' and refers to solid and dashed wave profiles at two times
wave speed must be inferred from visual wave-profile information not fully encoded in text
multi_step: requires combining two time states of the wave profile: t=0 and t=0.1 s
question adds an extra constraint about the particle at x=6 m not changing direction during the interval
solving likely needs visual interpretation plus physical reasoning to map profile shift to wave speed
multiple-choice format with 4 options suggests a single derived numeric result
verifiability: answer_text is a specific numeric result with unit: '10 m/s'
answer_type=short_text and choices are provided, enabling exact-match or option-level checking
the target quantity is well-defined (wave speed), so a derived solution can be checked against the stated answer

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are well supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
image dependency is very high, and the required figure is available
